rivers.explanation: Milwaukee River looks like a skip today. The gauge is above the low-water mark at 1,370 cfs, but the upper end is still uncertain. -140 cfs over the last 24h. The river is above its minimum for now, but falling water can quickly turn a minimum-only reach into a scrapy day. Wind looks noticeable at about 15 mph, with gusts near 17 mph. No major rain signal is showing up right now. Rain looks imminent in the next few hours. Recent rainfall is still running through this watershed (0.5 in the last 24h, 0.6 in the last 72h). Air temperature is 50°F, which lowers same-day trip quality more in the shoulder season. Confidence is medium. The access points and gauge are solid, and the low-water floor is specific. The remaining gap is a well-supported upper target rather than the basic go/no-go floor.

river.name: Milwaukee River
river.reach: Waubedonia Park to Veterans Memorial Park

rivers.explanation: Milwaukee River looks like a skip today. The gauge is above the low-water mark at 1,370 cfs, but the upper end is still uncertain. -140 cfs over the last 24h. The river is above its minimum for now, but falling water can quickly turn a minimum-only reach into a scrapy day. Wind looks noticeable at about 15 mph, with gusts near 16 mph. No major rain signal is showing up right now. Rain looks imminent in the next few hours. Some recent rainfall is still in play (0.4 in the last 24h). Air temperature is 50°F, which lowers same-day trip quality more in the shoulder season. Confidence is medium. The access points and direct gauge are solid. The weakest part is still the threshold model: the app can defend a same-gauge low-water floor and some high-water caution, but not a clean ideal band for this exact reach yet.

rivers.explanation: Wapsipinicon River looks like a skip today. The gauge is above the low-water mark at 12.91 ft, but the upper end is still uncertain. +1.14 ft over the last 24h. The trend is visible, but incomplete upper guidance keeps this call conservative. Wind looks manageable. Some rain is possible later today. Rain is likely later today. Air temperature is 41°F, which lowers same-day trip quality more in the shoulder season. Confidence is medium. The direct gauge and public access are solid. The best numeric support today is a low-water floor plus an ideal note from one detailed trip report, not a published upper range.

river.name: Kinnickinnic River
river.reach: Glen Park to Kinnickinnic State Park

rivers.explanation: Kinnickinnic River looks like a skip today. The gauge is still on the low side at 106 cfs. +1 cfs over the last 24h. The trend is not strong enough to move the call by itself. Wind looks noticeable at about 10 mph, with gusts near 18 mph. No major rain signal is showing up right now. Rain is likely later today. Air temperature is 36°F, which lowers same-day trip quality more in the shoulder season. Continuous swiftwater, shallow class II features, anglers, and the steep state-park carry keep this well above a casual beginner run. This kind of reach needs more margin even when the gauge is in range. Confidence is medium. The route and direct gauge are strong, and the low-water story is specific. The main remaining gap is a reliable upper range for when fast water stops being a fun Class II day.
